channel.title: AI & Tech News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregated AI and Tech news from 11 sources

item.title: 【钛晨报】金融监管总局最新部署！大型机构要带头维护县域金融秩序；宁德时代回应将联手张雪机车造电摩；特朗普威胁对向伊朗供武国家征收50%关税
item.description: [钛媒体] 阿里组织架构调整，通义实验室升级为事业部；腾讯上线首个浏览器“龙虾”QBotClaw；Anthropic在源代码泄露数日后推出网络安全AI模型；三星显示器据悉成苹果可折叠OLED独家供应商；国务院国资委成立境外国资工作局；工信部：将谋划引导太空算力建设应用的政策措施，加快太空算力产业生态培育。

item.title: Conflicting Rulings Leave Anthropic in ‘Supply-Chain Risk’ Limbo
item.description: [WIRED AI] A US appeals court ruling is at odds with a separate, lower court decision from March, leaving uncertainty about if and how the US military can use the AI company's Claude model.

item.title: US court declines to block Pentagon's Anthropic blacklisting for now
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: OpenAI will reserve portion of IPO shares for retail investors, CFO tells CNBC
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: AI agent Poke makes setting up automations as easy as sending a text
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Poke brings AI agents to everyday users via text message by handling tasks and automations without complex setup, apps, or technical know-how.

item.title: OpenAI made economic proposals — here’s what DC thinks of them
item.description: [The Verge AI] Happy ceasefire day and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge subscribers about Big Tech's rocky journey through the world of politics. If you're not a subscriber yet, you can do so here, but my only request is that you sign up before Donald Trump decides to revisit his previous threats towar

item.title: AWS boss explains why investing billions in both Anthropic and OpenAI is an OK conflict
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] AWS has an ingrained culture of handling competition, he explained, because the cloud giant also competes with its partners.

item.title: Applied Digital's quarterly net loss widens on rising costs, one-time charge
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Meta’s New AI Model Gives Mark Zuckerberg a Seat at the Big Kid’s Table
item.description: [WIRED AI] Muse Spark is Meta’s first model since its AI reboot, and the benchmarks suggest formidable performance.

item.title: Tubi is the first streamer to launch a native app within ChatGPT
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Tubi becomes the first streaming service to offer an app integration within ChatGPT, the AI chatbot that millions of users turn to for answers.

item.title: The US Army Is Building Its Own Chatbot for Combat
item.description: [WIRED AI] The AI system, trained on real military data, is meant to give soldiers mission-critical information.

item.title: 5 Burning Questions About Elon Musk’s Terafab Chip Partnership with Intel
item.description: [WIRED AI] Intel’s role in Elon Musk’s ambitious chip venture is still murky, raising questions about what the partnership actually entails—and whether it can work at all.

item.title: Anthropic’s New Product Aims to Handle the Hard Part of Building AI Agents
item.description: [WIRED AI] Amid rapid enterprise growth, Anthropic is trying to lower the barrier to entry for businesses to build AI agents with Claude.

item.title: Meta is reentering the AI race with a new model called Muse Spark
item.description: [The Verge AI] Meta Superintelligence Labs is launching its first model since Mark Zuckerberg spent billions overhauling the company's AI efforts. Called Muse Spark, the model now powers the Meta AI app and the Meta AI website in the US, per the company's announcement. In the coming weeks, Meta says, it will appea

item.title: Citigroup says AI helps speed account openings and systems upgrades
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Meta unveils first AI model from costly superintelligence team
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Astropad’s Workbench reimagines remote desktop for AI agents, not IT support
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Astropad’s Workbench lets users remotely monitor and control AI agents on Mac Minis from iPhone or iPad, with low-latency streaming and mobile access.

item.title: OpenAI releases a new safety blueprint to address the rise in child sexual exploitation
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] OpenAI's new Child Safety Blueprint aims to tackle the alarming rise in child sexual exploitation linked to advancements in AI.

item.title: Databricks co-founder wins prestigious ACM award, says ‘AGI is here already’
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Matei Zaharia has won the top honor from the Association for Computing Machinery. Now he's working on AI for research and says AGI is simply misunderstood.

item.title: 芯片出海，征服日本汽车？
item.description: [钛媒体] 境外市场主动拥抱“中国芯”。

item.title: 华裔领衔神秘小队，护航Anthropic“玻璃之翼”
item.description: [钛媒体] 从量子物理博士到Anthropic红队网络安全负责人。

item.title: The vibes are off at OpenAI
item.description: [The Verge AI] OpenAI is in a relatively precarious position. The company is and has been a funding behemoth - just over a week ago, it closed $122 billion in funding at a post-money valuation of $852 billion. It's potentially planning for an IPO later this year. ChatGPT's longtime lead in consumer-facing AI led i

item.title: ST Explains: What to consider before selling your Singtel special discounted shares
item.description: [Straits Times] About 460,000 of Singtel SDS holders do not own any ordinary shares from the telco.

item.title: Telenor faces lawsuit over claims it exposed Myanmar customers to junta repression
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Unionized ProPublica staff are on strike over AI, layoffs, and wages
item.description: [The Verge AI] Unionized staff at ProPublica, one of the country's leading nonprofit newsrooms, are walking off the job for 24 hours beginning Wednesday and asking the public to honor a digital picket line. The roughly 150 members of the ProPublica Guild are in the midst of negotiating a collective bargaining agre

item.title: 氪星晚报｜星巴克中国发布“千店千面”战略；TikTok宣布将投资10亿欧元，在芬兰建第二座数据中心；谷歌CEO皮查伊：2027年将是AI重塑生产方式的关键拐点
item.description: [36氪] 大公司： 三星电子中国业务陷重组传闻，重心转向半导体 4月8日，有消息称，三星电子中国战略将进行调整，未来可能只保留手机和存储业务，重心转向存储等半导体业务。此外，三星电子在中国的家电业务不断收缩，今年下半年有可能变为代理制。截至发稿前，三星官方并未对上述消息做出正式回应。（第一财经） 星巴克中国发布“千店千面”战略 36氪获悉，星巴克中国今日宣布，星巴克全球与博裕投资的战略合作已启动，并发布“千店千面”战略，聚焦五大举措，包括：专业咖啡首选、高品质产品创新、场景化门店拓展、伙伴打造“一店一社区”、AI助力人文联结。 上海迪士尼乐园自开园以来已累计接待游客超一亿人次 中

item.title: Greece to ban social media for under-15s from next year
item.description: [BBC Technology] It follows similar moves in other European countries, including France and Spain.

item.title: Singapore stocks rise after US-Iran ceasefire announcement; STI up 0.8%
item.description: [Straits Times] The benchmark Straits Times Index gained 38.04 points to finish at 4,996.05

item.title: 腾讯音乐的版权，挡不住汽水音乐的算法
item.description: [钛媒体] 新模式与旧时代的较量。

item.title: 面向AI的SSD，彻底火出圈
item.description: [钛媒体] 面向AI的SSD，进入产业狂欢。

item.title: GPT-6如果只是吊胃口，那OpenAI就真会掉下悬崖了
item.description: [钛媒体] 王牌，卡在决战前夜。

item.title: 阿里电商 AI 新动向：围绕 Token 重构电商，组织迎来新一轮调整｜独家
item.description: [36氪] 文｜彭倩 编辑｜乔芊 杨轩 4月1日新财年伊始，由蒋凡统领的阿里巴巴中国电商事业群正在规划可以结合 AI 实现的增长点。 据36氪了解，淘天集团当下对 AI 的探索将聚焦在 AI to B 方向上，其核心 OKR 是商家侧的 AI 工具留存率以及 AI 带来的 GMV 增长。它的背景是进入2026年， Open Claw 引爆 AI Agent，阿里 CEO 吴泳铭提出阿里组建新的 Alibaba Token Hub（ATH ）事业群，所有关联业务需要围绕 Token 进行商业化。 组织变动也随之发生。据36氪了解，中国电商事业群 AI 业务的负责人张凯夫不

item.title: 我们寻找迎风而立的人｜2026年「36Under36」 征集开始
item.description: [36氪] 出品｜36氪创投研究院 文｜罗婧、杜梦 如何形容2026的创投行业？ 「暗涌Waves」回望过去一年，试图找到最恰当的形容：当AI重新搭建产业底座，当硬科技成为底色，当全球化与本土创新彼此缠绕，我们已全然接受一级市场的新叙事、新世代。 从肖弘2016年的一场黑客松，到2026年的蝴蝶效应，我们深知总有人挺立在创业前线；当一位位科学家从实验室走向量产前线，我们坚信总有人在开辟前路。 这群人，有一个共同点：不被风口塑形，在湍流中站稳，用持续创造定义自己的形状。年轻，是他们的共性。每一位都用行动证明：真正的价值从来不在风口之上，而在长期坚持的土地里。 提炼今年的创投圈：风已至

item.title: CK Hutchison starts London arbitration against Maersk over Panama
item.description: [Straits Times] The move escalates the dispute over the Balboa and Cristobal ports along the strategic Panama Canal.

item.title: 36氪首发 | 柔性触觉传感器厂商完成近亿元融资，切入具身智能与锂电安全双场景
item.description: [36氪] 作者 | 乔钰杰 编辑 | 袁斯来 硬氪获悉，苏州灵动佳芯科技有限公司（下称“灵动佳芯”）近期完成A轮融资，融资金额近亿元。本轮融资引入锦秋基金、中芯聚源、瑞丞投资、润璋创投等多家产业方与财务投资机构，慕石资本担任长期战略财务顾问。 灵动佳芯成立于2021年，是一家基于压电材料与半导体技术开发传感器解决方案的企业，应用场景覆盖智能机器人、智能穿戴、新能源与锂电池等多个领域。 创始核心团队均出身于国内电子科技大厂，拥有数十年管理、研发及量产经验。 在业务路径上，公司自成立之初即采取“成熟业务+前沿技术”双线推进策略：一方面，通过成熟市场

item.title: 产品观察 | 追觅入局智能床垫赛道，3个月公司估值10亿
item.description: [36氪] 作者 | 乔钰杰 编辑 | 袁斯来 中国每年向全球出口价值100亿的床垫，但很长一段时间，智能床垫行业的明星公司并不诞生于此地。 这个行业的明星创业者，是一个来自意大利小城费拉拉的业余运动员。2014年，他在纽约和妻子成立了一家智能床垫公司Eight Sleep，如今每年卖出5亿美元，估值达到15亿美元。 毫无意外，Eight Sleep的代工厂和研发伙伴都在珠三角。在看到Eight Sleep让人惊讶的销量后，中国公司终于冲入这个市场。 今年年初，追觅生态旗下的智能床垫品牌希瑞（stareep）宣布完成第二轮近亿元融资，投后估值达到

item.title: No further action against Popiah King Sam Goi for breach of takeover code
item.description: [Straits Times] He says he misunderstood the provisions of the code, and his cooperation and remedial actions were factored in.

item.title: 8点1氪丨马斯克在对OpenAI的诉讼中寻求罢免奥特曼职位；宁德时代聘请紫金矿业创始人陈景河；产业链人士：苹果首款折叠屏手机项目正常推进，产品下半年发布
item.description:
[36氪] 今日热点导览
海康威视回应辟谣“监控系统出现漏洞，300多人被带走” 郭艾伦否认被骗千万 日本餐饮巨头食其家创始人心梗去世 产业链人士：苹果首款折叠屏手机项目正常推进 宇树科技移动机器人新专利获授权
TOP 3大新闻 嫣然天使儿童医院发文感谢陈光标捐赠1000万 4月7日，“北京嫣然天使儿童医院体检中心”官方账号发文感谢陈光标对嫣然医院的支持，并发布了颁发给陈光标的感谢证书。证书内文写到：感谢您对北京嫣然天使儿童医院的大力支持。因为你们的帮助，更多的孩子在这里重建笑容。我们坚信，唯有怀抱着善良与爱，才能在公益之路上勇敢前行！4月5日，陈光标

item.title: Elon Musk seeks removal of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman as trial looms
item.description: [Straits Times] Musk is suing OpenAI and Microsoft over claims that the ChatGPT maker abandoned its founding mission.

item.title: Intel joins Elon Musk’s Terafab AI chip project to power humanoid, data centre goals
item.description: [Straits Times] Intel’s shares jumped nearly 3 per cent after the announcement.

item.title: Spotify’s Prompted Playlists can help you find new podcasts to listen to
item.description: [The Verge AI] On Tuesday, Spotify expanded its Prompted Playlists feature to include podcasts, an update that could make it easier for Premium users to find new shows to listen to. Prompted Playlists were originally launched as a beta feature in December, but previously only worked for music. You can use the feat

item.title: Anthropic Teams Up With Its Rivals to Keep AI From Hacking Everything
item.description: [WIRED AI] The AI lab's Project Glasswing will bring together Apple, Google, and more than 45 other organizations. They'll use the new Claude Mythos Preview model to test advancing AI cybersecurity capabilities.

item.title: A new Anthropic model found security problems ‘in every major operating system and web browser’
item.description: [The Verge AI] Anthropic is debuting a new AI model as part of a cybersecurity partnership with Nvidia, Google, Amazon Web Services, Apple, Microsoft, and other companies. Project Glasswing, as it's called, is billed as a way for large companies, and potentially even the government, to flag vulnerabilities in thei

item.title: AI content can rank, but quality still decides winners
item.description: [MarTech] Semrush’s study shows AI content is already competing in search, but performance still comes down to execution, not the tool. The post AI content can rank, but quality still decides winners appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Ex-Meta worker investigated for downloading 30,000 private Facebook photos
item.description: [BBC Technology] The former employee was fired from Meta after being suspected of downloading 30,000 photos, the company said.

item.title: Anthropic tops US$30 billion run rate, seals deal with Broadcom
item.description: [Business Times] More than 1,000 business customers spend over US$1 million annually on Claude services

item.title: Using AI isn’t the same as getting value from it
item.description: [MarTech] Most teams have access to AI, but still struggle to see meaningful results. The issue isn’t the tools, but where they’re applied. The post Using AI isn’t the same as getting value from it appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: OpenAI encourages firms to trial four-day weeks to adapt to AI era
item.description: [BBC Technology] The ChatGPT-maker said its early policy ideas aim to prompt discussions about action needed as AI systems become more capable.

item.title: China is winning one AI race, the US another - but either might pull ahead
item.description: [BBC Technology] Both sides don't want to let their rival dominate. And the competition may yet be transformed further.

item.title: How China fell for a lobster: What an AI assistant tells us about Beijing's ambition
item.description: [BBC Technology] The AI agent sparked a frenzy of "raising lobsters" in March, with users training the tool to suit their needs.

item.title: Nasa astronauts reach safe orbit in historic moon mission
item.description: [Business Times] [AUSTIN] Nasa’s crew of astronauts launched to space and reached a stable orbit, kicking off a landmark journey that will take them...

item.title: Broadcom signs long-term deal to develop Google’s custom AI chips
item.description: [Business Times] Demand for custom chips has surged in recent years

item.title: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google unite to combat model copying in China
item.description: [Business Times] Trump administration officials have signalled their openness to fostering information sharing among AI companies to rein in adversarial distillation

item.title: Businesses scramble to get noticed by AI search
item.description: [BBC Technology] Firms are changing the way they present information on their websites, so they get noticed by AI.

item.title: AI and empathy define the next era of marketing systems
item.description: [MarTech] The real opportunity with AI is not scale alone, but designing experiences that reduce friction and support both customers and teams. The post AI and empathy define the next era of marketing systems appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Three first-party data strategies retail brands are prioritizing now
item.description: [MarTech] As 3rd-party cookies fade, mid-market retailers are leaning on direct value exchanges, ID-driven experiences and zero-party signals to rebuild their data. The post Three first-party data strategies retail brands are prioritizing now appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Why AI-driven creative is failing and how to fix it
item.description: [MarTech] From Coca-Cola to Svedka, poor execution is undermining AI-driven creative. Learn how to use genAI in ways that support storytelling. The post Why AI-driven creative is failing and how to fix it appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: AI adoption fails at the human level
item.description: [MarTech] The biggest blocker to AI isn’t technology. It’s fear, risk perception and past experience. Here’s how to address what’s really holding teams back. The post AI adoption fails at the human level appeared first on MarTech.
